title: Add quiescence loop tests to test_cli_run.py
description:
Append four tests to `tests/test_cli_run.py` that cover the quiescence loop behaviour. Each test mocks `Scheduler.run_once` directly (no FakeRunner needed) and invokes `command_run` via the existing test harness in that file.

1. `test_run_loops_until_quiescence`: `run_once` returns three SchedulerResult objects in sequence — `started=['B-1']`, `started=['B-2']`, `started=[]`. Assert `run_once` called exactly 3 times; final summary contains both bead IDs.
2. `test_run_stops_when_no_beads_started`: `run_once` returns `started=[]` immediately. Assert called exactly once.
3. `test_run_respects_max_cycles_cap`: `run_once` always returns `started=['B-N']`. With `--max-cycles 5`, assert called exactly 5 times and a warning was emitted.
4. `test_run_quiescence_with_deferred_only_exits`: `run_once` returns `started=[], deferred=['B-X']`. Assert loop exits after one call — deferred beads do not prevent quiescence.
acceptance_criteria: All four tests exist in `tests/test_cli_run.py` and pass individually with `uv run pytest tests/test_cli_run.py -k 'quiescence or max_cycles or deferred_only' -v`.
test_run_loops_until_quiescence: run_once call count is exactly 3.
test_run_stops_when_no_beads_started: run_once call count is exactly 1.
test_run_respects_max_cycles_cap: run_once call count is exactly 5 and a warning line is present in captured output.
Full suite still passes: `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q`.
